objective: Create publication-grade random and fixed-heuristic discovery baselines on preregistered bounded structural game-form targets while reserving an untouched Wave 70 learned-ranker holdout.
non_negotiables: This plan remains draft and pending implementation until a future immutable Partizan commit contains the preregistration and accepted registry, generator, and baseline code before Stage A outputs.
The complete target registry and Stage A, Stage B, and Wave 70 assignments are committed before Stage A proposal generation.

The candidate source is the 108-row clean reference atlas with SHA-256 58046bbcbb4644018d4bf31907fcd555220d2bb8e2a5f67607beb6f515883dbf, regenerated from Astralbase 1434fca1fc04d97798ec1b820c56f52f8014ccc7, Bitmesh ade3417a007b9c8392d8a153abc4b3ed23edf0aa, and Thermograph 1d9b6b01c3921aca8c2a8fb13972fee8a4de5041, using Partizan base 89c325d52a67bde4d6ac997f4527b7c56a119cf7.
A temporary atlas path is not evidence; the integrated freeze binds a durable artifact path, the atlas content hash, and the future Partizan commit.
Every pool and every baseline ordering is frozen and hashed before any verifier result is revealed.
Random and fixed-heuristic baselines rank the same frozen proposals and join to one complete verifier ledger.
Stage A is calibration only; Stage B is evidence and retains failed targets and negative outcomes.
The primary metric is macro DE@256 using symmetry-unique verified matches per verifier call.
The fixed heuristic uses proposal-only pre-verification fields and is locked before Stage A outcomes.
Wave 70 targets receive no proposal generation, verification, or tuning during Wave 69.
Wave 70 is NO-GO until a versioned leakage-audited semantic target representation replaces the opaque-digest-only ranker view.
The domain remains a bounded chess-derived board form, not arbitrary CGT equivalence or exact orthodox chess.
No learned-ranker, agency, temperature, aesthetic-performance, or Stiller/Elkies exactness claim is made in Wave 69.

tasks.prompt: Freeze the Wave 69 target-selection algorithm, A/B pools, baselines, metrics, failure policy, evidence boundary, and Wave 70 holdout before implementing or running the experiment.
outputs: Human-readable preregistration with explicit calibration and evidence boundaries
commands: python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_69_preregistered_discovery_baselines.json
python3 scripts/validate_waves.py
git diff --check
acceptance: The plan validates against the research network.
The preregistration fixes target selection, sample sizes, baselines, primary metric, gates, and holdout conduct.
The document remains draft until the future integrated implementation commit and does not attribute itself to the 89c325d base.
Unimplemented commands are labeled pending implementation rather than represented as passing gates.

tasks.prompt: Validate the content-addressed 108-row clean atlas boundary, then implement the preregistered replay-based 18-target registry, deterministic six-bin topology selection, and immutable Stage A/B/Wave 70 assignment without inspecting Wave 69 candidate outcomes.
outputs: Six targets each for Stage A, Stage B, and Wave 70
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — test the target registry builder and replay every selected target from four clean immutable commits
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — validate the committed registry hash, uniqueness, topology balance, and 6/6/6 split
acceptance: The durable source artifact has 108 rows and SHA-256 58046bbcbb4644018d4bf31907fcd555220d2bb8e2a5f67607beb6f515883dbf and binds the declared clean source commits.
No temporary path or historical workspace provenance is promoted as evidence.
Rows exposed in Wave 68 are excluded before binning.
Each split has two targets from each of the three topology families.
All targets bind the bounded rule and SHA-256 structural identity.

tasks.prompt: Implement deterministic target-blind candidate generation, the seven preregistered proposal features, the total 20-times-requested-rows attempt cap, and byte-identical pool reproduction without invoking or observing the verifier.
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — run generator unit, determinism, feature-boundary, and attempt-cap tests
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — generate the same fixture pool twice and require byte identity
acceptance: Generation has no verifier dependency or verifier-derived feature.
Seeds follow the preregistered SHA-256 derivation.
The generator either fills the unique pool within one total cap of 20 times the requested unique rows or reports failure without padding.
The fixed feature names and definitions do not change after Stage A.

tasks.prompt: Implement the 1,000 seeded random permutations, frozen structural-heuristic ordering, DE metrics, uncertainty summaries, and pre-verification ordering manifests exactly as preregistered.
outputs: DE@64, DE@256, DE@1024, curve-area, first-success, and outcome-rate evaluator
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — run baseline ordering, seed, tie-break, metric, and leakage tests
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — prove that ordering hashes are independent of verifier-result bytes
acceptance: The primary metric is macro symmetry-unique DE@256.
The heuristic formula and candidate-key tie break match the preregistration.
All ordering artifacts can be frozen before verification.
No learned parameters or result-derived fields enter either baseline.

tasks.prompt: From clean immutable commits, freeze six 1,024-row Stage A pools and all baseline orderings, run one complete Astralbase verifier pass, and report the preregistered integrity and support gates.
outputs: Six frozen Stage A pools and manifests
Stage A calibration and support report
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — generate each Stage A pool twice, freeze all hashes, and audit the proposal-only boundary before verification
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — verify every frozen proposal once and validate complete result-ledger replay
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — compute the Stage A integrity and per-target support gates
acceptance: Each target has exactly 1,024 unique frozen proposals or the stage stops before verification.
All orderings predate verifier results.
There is one result per proposal and zero internal errors.
Stage A is labeled calibration-only and is not used as paper evidence.

tasks.prompt: Independently recompute Stage A hashes, ordering independence, ledger coverage, metrics, and support thresholds. Issue GO only if all six targets pass without replacement or selective retry.
outputs: GO or no-go decision for Stage B
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — independently replay Stage A and compare every pool, ordering, result, and report hash
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — scan ranker inputs and git history for result leakage or post-outcome artifact changes
acceptance: All integrity gates are recomputed independently.
Every target meets 95% verified coverage, eight matches, and four symmetry-unique matches.
Any failure produces a documented no-go rather than target substitution.
No Stage B artifact is generated before a GO audit.

tasks.prompt: After a documented Stage A GO, freeze six independent 4,096-row Stage B pools and all orderings before one complete verifier pass, then compute every preregistered result without dropping targets or outcomes.
outputs: Six frozen Stage B pools and manifests
Preregistered per-target and macro baseline report
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — freeze and audit all Stage B pools and orderings before verifier access
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — execute one complete non-adaptive verifier pass and replay the ledger
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — compute the fixed heuristic-minus-random DE@256 contrast and all locked secondary metrics
acceptance: Every Stage B target remains in the report regardless of yield.
Every call, rejection, nonmatch, duplicate realization, and error remains in the denominator and ledger.
The primary result is fixed heuristic DE@256 minus random-mean DE@256.
A support miss is reported as a valid no-go rather than repaired post hoc.

tasks.prompt: Audit the Stage B evidence against the preregistration, report positive or no-go outcomes without target filtering, preserve the bounded-domain claim, and confirm that no Wave 70 pool was generated or inspected.
commands: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — independently recompute Stage B hashes, ledgers, metrics, randomization tail, and target-bootstrap interval
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — verify that all Wave 70 targets remain proposal- and outcome-unseen
PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — audit every manuscript-facing sentence against the bounded structural-tree evidence boundary
acceptance: The report labels Stage A calibration and Stage B evidence.
The report includes all six targets and all preregistered primary and secondary metrics.
A positive efficiency claim is blocked unless the Stage B support threshold passes.
No claim implies arbitrary CGT equivalence, orthodox-chess exactness, or learned discovery.
The six Wave 70 targets remain untouched for the learned-ranker phase.
Wave 70 remains NO-GO until verifier-red-team approval of a semantic, canonical, leakage-audited ranker-view contract.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_69_preregistered_discovery_baselines.json && python3 scripts/validate_waves.py && git diff --check
integration_gates.success_signal: The current Wave 69 plan and frozen preregistration are internally consistent.

integration_gates.command: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — build and validate the immutable 18-target registry before any Wave 69 candidate generation
integration_gates.success_signal: Six Stage A, six Stage B, and six Wave 70 targets replay and satisfy the preregistered deterministic split.

integration_gates.command: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — validate Stage A frozen pools, pre-verification orderings, complete ledger, and support report
integration_gates.success_signal: All six Stage A targets pass integrity, 95% verified coverage, eight matches, and four symmetry-unique matches.

integration_gates.command: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — validate and independently recompute the complete Stage B evidence bundle
integration_gates.success_signal: The preregistered Stage B baseline contrast and secondary metrics recompute for all six targets without leakage or filtering.

integration_gates.command: PENDING IMPLEMENTATION — audit that no Wave 70 proposal pool, result, or tuning artifact exists and approve a semantic leakage-audited target ranker view before the future model freeze
integration_gates.success_signal: The six-target Wave 70 holdout remains untouched and learned ranking stays NO-GO until the semantic-target contract passes red-team review.
